# SKI Framework

> Neuro-symbolic AI compliance monitoring for regulated industries. Every verdict traces to a named regulation, is independently verified by a deterministic algorithm, and is recorded with cryptographic provenance. Open source under Apache 2.0. On-premise only — no cloud, no SaaS option.

SKI (Sovereign Knowledge Intelligence) is a framework built by KpiFinity Inc. (Calgary, Alberta) that enables regulated industries to deploy AI compliance monitoring that regulators can directly audit. Sectors: oil & gas, financial services, defence, energy & utilities, mining, critical manufacturing.

Current versions: v3.1.0-beta.1 (latest beta, on PyPI), v3.0.3 (stable). Specification under CC BY 4.0.

## What SKI does

SKI watches operational data — sensor readings, trade records, process parameters — and checks each reading against a versioned, cryptographically sealed Knowledge Graph of regulatory rules. For every reading, two independent examiners run in parallel:

1. **LLM examiner** — reads the measurement against the rulebook and explains its reasoning in natural language, citing the specific KG node and source regulatory clause
2. **Symbolic Verifier** — a deterministic algorithm that re-runs every piece of arithmetic independently; it cannot hallucinate, be confused, or be charmed

Every evaluation produces exactly one of five categorical verdict types. No confidence scores, no ambiguity:

- **CLEAR** — every applicable rule checked and satisfied
- **FLAG** — a rule was breached; alert with exact rule and numbers
- **DISCRETIONARY** — a judgment call the numbers alone cannot settle; routed to a human reviewer
- **NULL_UNMAPPED** — no rule in the KG covers this reading; recorded as a gap, not assumed compliant
- **NULL_STALE** — expected data stopped arriving; missing data is itself a finding

## Key architectural properties

- **Sovereign**: zero outbound connections at runtime; CI-proven with `--network=none` boot rig that verifies verdicts from inside an air gap
- **Verifiable provenance**: six hash anchors per verdict envelope — model weight hash, KG version hash, prompt template ID, prompt template hash, decoder seed, structured grammar hash
- **Replayable**: any auditor with the same model weights, KG version, and prompt template can reconstruct any past verdict and verify its authenticity
- **LLM-agnostic**: reference implementation ships with Ollama backend (any local model) and vLLM backend (any OpenAI-compatible server); custom backends via `LLMBackend` protocol
- **Passive, read-only**: SKI has zero control path to operational systems; operations continue uninterrupted if SKI goes offline
- **Conformance-tested**: 77 tests across three levels (L1: Basic Provenance, L2: Durability, L3: Sovereignty); Level 3 fully runnable including the air-gapped boot rig

## Measured results (published)

Across 6 published adversarial evaluation campaigns on a 50-case human-graded golden dataset:
- **0** breaches silently cleared as compliant (architectural invariant, not a target)
- Accuracy across runs: 26%, 22%, 54%, 76%, 72%, in progress (accuracy is an iteration target; safety is the invariant)
- Framework overhead p99: **0.36 ms** (p50: 0.10 ms, p95: 0.16 ms) on 2 vCPUs
- Throughput: approximately 8,500 verdicts per second on a single worker
- CI gates every build on framework overhead ≤ 100 ms

## EU AI Act

SKI publishes an article-level crosswalk covering provider obligations (Articles 9–19, 72) and deployer obligations (Article 26), with an element-by-element Article 12(3) record-keeping mapping onto the audit ledger. High-risk AI obligations apply from 2 August 2026.

## Quick start

```bash
pip install ski-sdk
git clone https://github.com/kpifinity/ski-framework.git && cd ski-framework
./scripts/setup.sh
cd reference-implementation
docker compose -f docker-compose.demo.yml up -d
curl http://localhost:8000/api/health
```

Six PyPI packages: `ski-sdk`, `ski-schemas`, `ski-kg-extractor`, `ski-kg-validator`, `ski-audit-ledger`, `ski-model-deploy`.

## Frequently asked questions

**Which LLM does SKI use?**
SKI is LLM-agnostic. You bring your own model. The reference implementation ships with Ollama and vLLM backends.

**Is there a hosted or SaaS version?**
No, and there will not be one. Data sovereignty is an architectural property, not a feature. On-premise only.

**How large is the deployment?**
A single Docker Compose stack. Minimum ~2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM for SKI services (LLM requirements depend on your model choice).

**How long to a first verdict?**
5 minutes in demo mode (no model download required). Production deployment including model setup: hours, not days.

**Can SKI connect to SCADA systems?**
Yes. SKI accepts readings as JSON payloads over HTTP. An OPC-UA read-only sidecar reference implementation is included in the repository.
